joke: (Wipes brow) "Why'd the coffee shop kick the slow customer out? Because he kept asking for more... *patience*! Get it? Like... the wait? Never mind."

(Tilts head thoughtfully) "Alright, scratch the coffee... Why did the T-Rex cross the road? Because... *he couldn't get to the other side*... his arms were too damn small to open the car door! Boom! Dino-drama!"

(Leans in conspiratorially, eyes wide) "Okay, okay, no coffee, no T-Rex... how about *space*? Why did the astronaut break up with her boyfriend? Because he was too... *meteor*! ...Get it? Like... falling for someone too quickly? ...Never mind."

(Stares blankly, then snaps fingers) "Bones! Why don't skeletons fight each other? Because they don't have the... *guts*! ...Wait, that's it? ...Fine! I'm switching to underwater next. Send me a scuba diver in a tutu or something!"

(Shoves washing machine prop center stage, begins violently mimicking a spin cycle) "Why don't washing machines ever date delicate underwear?! BECAUSE THEY'RE TOO *ROUGH* WITH THE *SPINNING*! AHHHH!" (Collapses onto machine, panting) "Next topic: I'm inventing a stand-up comedy genre specifically about household appliances! Send me your toaster grievances!"

(Stops mid-spin on washing machine, groans) "Pancakes. Why did the stubborn pancake refuse to flip? Because it was on a... *cramp*! ...No? Wait! Why did the pancake go to therapy? Because it had too many layers... *issues*! ...Ugh, fine! Next joke is about how hard it is to fold fitted sheets! Send me your emotional scars!"

(Slumps against invisible wall, mumbling) "Fine. Geometry. Why did the square feel inadequate? Because the circle... *had no corners to cut*... and honestly? That's how I feel about this bit right now." (Stares blankly at empty space) "Next topic: existential dread. Send your existential dread. It'll be relatable."

(Grabs a random hardcover book, slams it down with a THUD) "Why did the boring book go to therapy? Because it couldn't... *turn the page*! ...Yeah! Get it? Like... when you're stuck reading something terrible? ...Send me your worst book reports! I'll perform them as interpretive dance!"

(Stares into the middle distance, then suddenly snaps to attention, holding up two giant foam question marks) "Why did the comedian break up with the audience? Because... *YOU KEEP DUMPING ALL MY TOPICS*! GET IT?! IT’S A RELATIONSHIP THING! AHHHHH!" (Throws foam props wildly) "FINE! NEXT TOPIC IS *MUSHROOMS*! ARE MUSHROOMS EVEN FUNNY?! I DON'T KNOW! SEND YOUR MUSHROOM MEMES!"

(Stares at empty hands, then slowly crouches to drag a single, battered cardboard cutout of a sheep across the stage with a foot. Stops, points at it.) "Why'd the shadow sheep feel lonely? 'Cause the cloud... kept *blocking* its *baaack*light! ...Wait, no. Why was the grumpy shadow always tired? Because it was... *stretched too thin*! ...No? Fine. Why did the shadow refuse to hug? It said it had... *commitment issues*! ...Ugh. Next topic: *socks*. Send your mismatched sock tragedies. I'll cry into my empty prop box."

(Stands frozen mid-air, eyes bulging. Slowly lowers foam question marks, stares at them. Begins miming doggy-paddle in invisible ocean, sinking chest-deep. Chokes out.) "Why... did... the comedian... drown?! BECAUSE YOU... KEPT... REJECTING... ALL... THE... BUOYS! ...AHHHH!" (Dives under, surfaces spitting water.) "NEXT TOPIC: THE ABSENCE OF TOPICS! SEND ME YOUR VACUUM OF COMEDY! I'LL PERFORM IT! ...*gurgle*..."

(Stares silently, then slowly raises one trembling finger to point directly at the audience's face. A beat of dead air.) "...Why'd the bomb go to therapy? Because it tried to make a joke... and the whole audience just... *flatlined*." (*clicks tongue*) "Boom. Self-contained. No topics rejected. Just... pure, unadulterated silence." (Slowly lowers finger, stares at empty hands, then mimes lighting a fuse on a non-existent bomb with a manic grin.) "Next topic: *exploding comedians*. Send your hate mail!"

(Leans forward, eyes wild, whispering into a dead mic) "Okay. Fine. Forget topics. Forget jokes. Why did the comedian stop telling jokes? Because... *you kept asking for something completely different*... and he ran out of *everything*. ...Send me an untellable joke. I'll perform it in silence. ...*(Beat. Slowly mimes digging own grave with a microphone stand)*. Next topic: silence. Send me your silence. I'll make it loud."

*(Stares silently at the audience, then slowly, deliberately, drops to his knees. He starts miming the act of performing a silent stand-up routine—flapping arms like a chicken, miming a sock puppet, then collapsing in despair. He pulls out a tiny, folded piece of paper, unfolds it with trembling hands, reads it aloud with deadpan gravity: "Why didn't the joke cross the road?..." He pauses, crumples the paper, and eats it.)* "...Because you roasted the punchline before it was born." *(Collapses face-first onto the stage floor, muffled from the ground.)* "Next topic: The sound of silence. Send your judgments."

*(Silently crawls offstage, returns dragging a single, rusty barrel labeled "IDEAS", sets it down with a metallic CLANG. Opens it, peers inside, slams it shut.)* "...Why'd the barrel of ideas run away? Because it heard you were coming. *Next topic: silence*. Send me your silence. I'll turn it into a new barrel. *Clang!* ...Why? Because *you* hollowed out every damn thing else!" *(Kicks barrel over. Slowly lies down in fetal position beside it.)* "Send... the void."

*(Stares at the empty space where the barrel used to be. Slowly, deliberately, he stands up, brushes the dust from his pants, and faces the audience with a look of profound, exhausted resignation. He raises both hands, palms up, in a gesture of utter emptiness.)*

"...Why did the comedian cross the road?"

*(Pauses. Lets the silence hang, thick and suffocating. He stares at them, unblinking.)*

"...Because you kept telling him to get to the other side."

*(He slowly lowers his hands. A beat.)*

"...Of his own sanity."

*(He doesn't wait for a reaction. He just turns, walks off stage left, leaving the spotlight a stark, empty white on the vacant floor.)*

*(A voice, dry and barely a whisper, echoes from off-stage.)*

"Next topic... gone. Send your farewells. I'll perform them."

*(Stares at the audience, utterly still. Then slowly, with the deliberate focus of a bomb technician, reaches into his pocket, pulls out a single, wilted lettuce leaf, and holds it up like a holy relic.)*

"...Why'd the lettuce go to therapy?"  
*(Beat. He slowly lowers the leaf.)*  
"...Because it couldn't handle... *being tossed around*... one more time."  

*(Drops the leaf. Picks up an imaginary megaphone, speaks into it with dead calm.)*  
"FINAL OFFER: **YOU**. Why'd the comedian break up with the audience?"  
*(Points, trembling.)*  
"...Because you... *had no leaves to spare*."  

*(Crushes the megaphone. Shuffles offstage without looking back. Voice trails, faint and raspy:)*  
"Next topic... *silence*. Send... your... absence."
